title: San Diego Speedboat Rental: Guide, Prices & Tips for 2026
html:
Renting a speedboat in San Diego lets you explore the Pacific coastline and Mission Bay at your own pace. A half-day self-drive hire from a reputable operator typically costs between $450 and $700 USD, and BoatHire24 offers a straightforward platform to find and book verified boats for your trip.

Where to set off in San Diego

San Diego Bay and Mission Bay are the two main departure zones for speedboat hire, each with several well-equipped marinas. In San Diego Bay, you can launch from Shelter Island Marina, Sun Harbor Marina, or Coronado Ferry Landing. For the calmer waters of Mission Bay, head to Bahia Resort Marina or Campland on the Bay. Each offers parking, fuel docks, and easy access to open water. You can browse boats for your dates in a few minutes.

From Shelter Island it is only 3 nautical miles to the Coronado Bridge, a popular landmark to cruise under. If you prefer a longer run, Point Loma lies 5 nautical miles west and offers sea-lion colonies and views of the downtown skyline.

How much does it cost to rent a speedboat in San Diego?

Prices vary by boat size, engine power, and rental duration, but expect to pay from $350 for a four-hour rental of a 19-foot bowrider up to $900 for a full day on a 25-foot deck boat. Peak season runs June through August, when demand pushes rates up by roughly 20 per cent. You can browse boats for your dates on BoatHire24 to compare availability and secure the best price.

  • Shared speedboat tour (2 hours): from $90 per person
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours, self-drive): $350, $500
  • Catamaran full-day (skippered, 6 guests): $1,200, $1,800
  • Crewed yacht (full-day, 12 guests): from $2,500

Which boat to rent

Choosing the right speedboat depends on your group size and whether you want to waterski, fish, or just cruise. The QuickSilver Activ 605 Open is a versatile 20-footer with a 150 HP engine, ideal for families of up to eight. You can book this speedboat via BoatHire24 and check its features before you commit. One option worth a look is this speedboat.

  1. Bowrider (19, 22 ft), Best for day cruising and watersports, seats 6, 8, from $400 half-day
  2. Deck boat (23, 26 ft), Stable platform for groups, seats 10, 12, from $600 half-day
  3. Cuddy cabin (20, 24 ft), Overnight-capable with basic berth, seats 4, 6, from $500 half-day
  4. Centre console (22, 26 ft), Fishing-oriented with open deck, seats 8, 10, from $550 half-day
  5. RIB (15, 18 ft), Agile and fast for two to four people, from $300 half-day

Do you need a licence to rent a speedboat in San Diego?

California requires a boating safety certificate for any person born on or after 1 January 1988 who operates a vessel with more than 10 horsepower. However, many rental operators provide a quick on-water familiarisation and do not check the card for a short rental. Always confirm with the operator before booking; they will specify the exact requirements for your chosen boat.

If you have never driven a boat before, book an hour-long briefing at the marina. Most San Diego operators, such as those at Sun Harbor Marina, offer a free quick start lesson with your rental.

What is the minimum age to drive a rental speedboat in San Diego?

You must be at least 18 years old to sign a rental agreement, and anyone under 18 may not operate the boat unless accompanied by a licensed adult.

Can I moor the speedboat overnight at a San Diego marina?

Some marinas allow overnight tie-ups for an extra fee, but most rental agreements require the boat to be returned by sunset or before the marina closes. You can also see more at browse the listings.

Does the rental price include fuel?

Many San Diego speedboat rentals charge fuel separately, you pay for what you use at the pump on return. Others include a full tank and expect it back full.

What happens if the weather turns bad?

Operators typically cancel or reschedule if sustained winds exceed 20 knots or visibility drops below one nautical mile; check the policy when you book.

At a glance

  • Price band: $350, $900 half-day (self-drive)
  • Peak months: June, August
  • Trip length: 4, 8 hours typical
  • Guest capacity: 6, 12 depending on boat
  • Currency: USD

Questions about boat rental in San Diego

Can I rent a speedboat for a whole day in San Diego?

Yes, full-day rentals (8 hours) are widely available for bowriders and deck boats, typically costing $700, $1,200.

Is it possible to rent a speedboat without a skipper?

You can rent a speedboat as a bareboat (self-drive) in San Diego, provided you meet the operator's age and experience requirements.

Which areas are best for snorkelling from a speedboat?

The protected coves of La Jolla, especially La Jolla Cove and the ecological reserve, offer calm water and abundant marine life for snorkelling.

Do I need to bring my own life jackets?

No, rental boats come with sufficient US Coast Guard, approved life jackets for all passengers, including children.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in San Diego or list your own. If you own a speedboat and live near the bay, you can earn extra income by offering it for charter. Start by creating a listing on the platform to reach thousands of potential renters. To find your next day on the water, browse the listings on BoatHire24.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in San Diego and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
